description: The fastest vegetable you can grow — ready to eat in just 4 weeks! Radishes are perfect for beginners and impatient gardeners. They need very little space and grow happily in shallow pots or window boxes.

characteristics.sunRequirement: Partial shade to full sun (3–6 hours)
characteristics.waterNeeds: Regular — even moisture for best roots

care.sowOutdoors: Sow seeds 1cm deep, 2–3cm apart, directly into containers from late March. Keep moist. Seedlings appear in 3–5 days.
care.plantOut: No transplanting needed — sow directly where they'll grow. Radishes don't like being moved.
care.watering: Keep evenly moist. Inconsistent watering causes radishes to crack or become woody and hollow.
care.feeding: No feeding needed — radishes grow too fast to benefit from it.
care.harvest: Pull up when the root is about 2–3cm across (check by brushing soil away from the top). Don't leave them too long or they go woody.
care.overwintering: Not applicable — sow winter radish varieties (mooli) in late summer for autumn harvest.

propagation.seedDescription: Round, reddish-brown seeds about 3mm across. Very easy to handle and sow. Stay viable for 4+ years.

deadheading.notes: Not applicable — harvest before flowering. If radishes bolt and flower, the roots become inedible, but the flowers are edible and tasty in salads.

seedSaving.method: Let a few radishes bolt and flower. After flowering, seed pods form — wait until they turn brown and dry, then pick and crack open to collect seeds.
seedSaving.seedPodDescription: Long, pointed seed pods that rattle when dry. Each pod contains several round seeds.

commonProblems: Woody/pithy roots — left in the ground too long. Harvest promptly when ready.
Flea beetle — tiny holes in leaves. Cover with fine mesh or fleece.
Splitting — caused by uneven watering. Keep soil consistently moist.
funFact: Radishes were so valued in ancient Greece that gold replicas were made of them as offerings to the god Apollo!
